env.name: CODEX_HOME
env.description: Moves the main Codex home/config root.

env.name: CODEX_SQLITE_HOME
env.description: Moves SQLite-backed Codex state.

global.notes: Codex also supports profile and system config layers; this registry leaf models the user-level home config.

local.notes: Project .codex config layers are loaded only for trusted projects.

global.notes: Codex discovers user skills from $CODEX_HOME/skills (deprecated but kept for backward compat) and $HOME/.agents/skills.

local.notes: Project skills are discovered from .codex/skills and .agents/skills between the project root and cwd.

global.notes: Codex has no native commands directory; external agent commands (e.g. .claude/commands) are migrated into .agents/skills as skills.

local.notes: Codex has no native commands directory; external agent commands are migrated into .agents/skills.

global.notes: Subagent role files are discovered from $CODEX_HOME/agents/*.toml.

local.notes: Project-level subagent role files are discovered from .codex/agents/*.toml.

global.notes: $HOME/.agents/skills is a user-level skills discovery root.

local.notes: .agents/skills is a project-level skills discovery root.

env.name: OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR
env.description: Overrides the main config directory.

env.name: OPENCODE_CONFIG
env.description: Points at a specific config file.

env.name: XDG_CONFIG_HOME
env.description: Redirects the XDG config root used by OpenCode.

env.name: XDG_DATA_HOME
env.description: Redirects the XDG data root used by OpenCode.

env.name: XDG_STATE_HOME
env.description: Redirects the XDG state root used by OpenCode.

env.name: XDG_CACHE_HOME
env.description: Redirects the XDG cache root used by OpenCode.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

local.notes: Project configuration is split between opencode.json and the directory-backed .opencode surfaces.

global.notes: OpenCode documents plural directory names; singular aliases remain backward-compatible but are not modeled separately.

local.notes: OpenCode documents plural directory names; singular aliases remain backward-compatible but are not modeled separately.

global.notes: OpenCode discovers skills from ~/.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md as an agent-compatible path.

local.notes: OpenCode discovers skills from .agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md as an agent-compatible path.

env.name: CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
env.description: Moves the Claude Code config/session root.

env.name: CLAUDE_CODE_DEBUG_LOGS_DIR
env.description: Moves Claude Code debug logs.

env.name: CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR
env.description: Moves Claude Code plugin cache.

global.notes: Claude Code documents one shared user settings file for both the CLI and IDE integrations.

local.notes: Project settings apply after the workspace trust flow; settings.local.json is intended for personal overrides.

global.notes: Anthropic documents .claude/commands as a legacy-compatible path alongside the newer skills system.

local.notes: Anthropic documents .claude/commands as a legacy-compatible path alongside the newer skills system.

global.notes: Claude Code discovers skills only from ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.claude/commands/. No support for the cross-tool ~/.agents/skills path.

local.notes: Claude Code discovers skills only from .claude/skills/ and .claude/commands/. No support for the cross-tool .agents/skills path.

env.name: GEMINI_CLI_HOME
env.description: Moves the Gemini CLI root; .gemini is created under it.

env.name: GEMINI_CLI_SYSTEM_DEFAULTS_PATH
env.description: Overrides system defaults file path.

env.name: GEMINI_CLI_SYSTEM_SETTINGS_PATH
env.description: Overrides system settings file path.

env.name: GEMINI_CLI_TRUSTED_FOLDERS_PATH
env.description: Overrides the trusted folders file path.

global.notes: When GEMINI_CLI_HOME is set, the documented settings still resolve under the nested .gemini directory.

local.notes: Project settings are documented at .gemini/settings.json even when GEMINI_CLI_HOME relocates the enclosing home.

global.notes: Gemini CLI discovers user skills from ~/.gemini/skills/ and the ~/.agents/skills/ alias.

local.notes: Workspace skills are discovered from .gemini/skills/ and the .agents/skills/ alias.

global.notes: Custom slash commands are loaded from .toml files in ~/.gemini/commands/.

local.notes: Project-level custom commands are loaded from .toml files in <project>/.gemini/commands/.

global.notes: Subagents are discovered from ~/.gemini/agents; enabled via experimental.enableAgents (default true).

local.notes: Subagents are discovered from .gemini/agents; the /agents reload command rescans both ~/.gemini/agents and .gemini/agents.

global.notes: ~/.agents/skills/ is an alias for user-level skills discovery.

local.notes: .agents/skills/ is an alias for workspace-level skills discovery.

env.name: AIDER_INPUT_HISTORY_FILE
env.description: Overrides the prompt input history file.

env.name: AIDER_CHAT_HISTORY_FILE
env.description: Overrides the chat history file.

env.name: AIDER_LLM_HISTORY_FILE
env.description: Overrides the LLM interaction log file.

env.name: AIDER_ENV_FILE
env.description: Overrides the env file aider reads for secrets.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: This leaf covers the YAML config file; aider's separate .env loading is documented but not modeled as support.config.

local.notes: Aider searches home, git-root, and current-directory config files; this registry anchors local support to ${CWD}.

global.notes: Aider has no skills system; conventions are loaded as plain markdown files via --read or /read-only.

local.notes: Aider has no skills system; conventions are loaded as plain markdown files via --read or /read-only.

global.notes: Aider has only built-in slash commands (/add, /commit, /lint, etc.); no user-defined command files.

local.notes: Aider has only built-in slash commands (/add, /commit, /lint, etc.); no user-defined command files.

global.notes: Aider has no subagent or custom-agent system; uses a single model with architect/editor mode.

local.notes: Aider has no subagent or custom-agent system; uses a single model with architect/editor mode.

global.notes: Aider does not support the .agents/skills standard.

local.notes: Aider does not support the .agents/skills standard.

env.name: GOOSE_PATH_ROOT
env.description: Moves all Goose config/data/state roots under one custom root.

env.name: GOOSE_RECIPE_PATH
env.description: Adds or overrides recipe discovery paths.

env.name: XDG_CONFIG_HOME
env.description: Redirects Goose config root on Unix-like systems.

env.name: XDG_DATA_HOME
env.description: Redirects Goose data root on Unix-like systems.

env.name: XDG_STATE_HOME
env.description: Redirects Goose state root on Unix-like systems.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: Goose stores multiple files under its config directory; this leaf models the documented config and permission files.

local.notes: Goose also documents project .goosehints files; this registry currently models the project recipe library path.

global.notes: Goose has no SKILL.md-based skills system; recipes are the primary customization mechanism.

local.notes: Goose has no SKILL.md-based skills system; recipes are the primary customization mechanism.

global.notes: Goose has built-in CLI commands (session, configure, run) but no user-defined command files.

local.notes: Goose has built-in CLI commands (session, configure, run) but no user-defined command files.

global.notes: Goose supports subagents via sub-recipes (ad-hoc agent definitions in YAML), not a dedicated agents directory.

local.notes: Goose supports subagents via sub-recipes (ad-hoc agent definitions in YAML), not a dedicated agents directory.

global.notes: Goose does not support the .agents/skills standard; uses recipes instead.

local.notes: Goose does not support the .agents/skills standard; uses recipes instead.

env.name: CLINE_DIR
env.description: Overrides the Cline CLI configuration/data directory.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

local.notes: Project configuration lives in .cline/ at the repository root.

global.notes: Skills use SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter; auto-discovered and invocable as slash commands.

local.notes: Skills use SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter; auto-discovered and invocable as slash commands.

global.notes: Built-in slash commands (/newtask, /smol, /newrule, /deep-planning, /reportbug) plus skills invocable as /skill-name.

local.notes: Built-in slash commands (/newtask, /smol, /newrule, /deep-planning, /reportbug) plus skills invocable as /skill-name.

global.notes: Global agent definitions stored in ~/.cline/agents/.

local.notes: Project agent definitions stored in .cline/agents/.

global.notes: Cline reads ~/.agents/AGENTS.md for rules but does not discover skills from ~/.agents/skills/.

local.notes: Cline reads AGENTS.md for rules but does not discover skills from .agents/skills/.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: Skills use SKILL.md; ~/.roo/skills/ has higher priority than ~/.agents/skills/.

local.notes: Skills use SKILL.md; .roo/skills/ has higher priority than .agents/skills/.

global.notes: Markdown files with optional frontmatter; filename becomes /command-name.

local.notes: Markdown files with optional frontmatter; filename becomes /command-name.

global.notes: Roo Code uses custom modes (custom_modes.yaml) instead of a dedicated agents directory.

local.notes: Roo Code uses project modes (.roomodes file) instead of a dedicated agents directory.

global.notes: Roo Code discovers skills from ~/.agents/skills/ as cross-agent shared locations.

local.notes: Roo Code discovers skills from .agents/skills/ as cross-agent shared locations.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: Skills are SKILL.md-based with frontmatter (name, description); loaded from ~/.continue/skills/ and also reads ~/.claude/skills/

local.notes: Skills loaded from .continue/skills/ in workspace; also reads .claude/skills/

global.notes: Slash commands loaded from .prompt and .md files in ~/.continue/prompts/ and ~/.continue/rules/

local.notes: Slash commands loaded from .prompt and .md files in .continue/prompts/ and .continue/rules/

global.notes: Agent definitions (YAML/Markdown) loaded from ~/.continue/agents/

local.notes: Agent definitions (YAML/Markdown) loaded from .continue/agents/

global.notes: Continue does not discover skills from ~/.agents/skills/; reads ~/.continue/skills/ and ~/.claude/skills/ instead

local.notes: Continue does not discover skills from .agents/skills/; reads .continue/skills/ and .claude/skills/ instead

env.name: Q_CLI_DATA_DIR
env.description: Overrides Amazon Q CLI local data directory.

env.name: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR
env.description: Can affect Amazon Q log placement on Linux.

env.name: TMPDIR
env.description: Can affect Amazon Q log placement.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: Amazon Q CLI has no SKILL.md-based skills system; uses rules (.amazonq/rules/**/*.md) and prompts for customization

local.notes: Amazon Q CLI has no SKILL.md-based skills system; uses rules (.amazonq/rules/**/*.md) and prompts for customization

global.notes: Custom prompts loaded from ~/.aws/amazonq/prompts/; invocable as @<prompt-name> or /prompts get <name>

local.notes: Custom prompts loaded from .amazonq/prompts/; invocable as @<prompt-name> or /prompts get <name>

global.notes: Agent definitions (JSON) loaded from ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents/; global agents available from any directory

local.notes: Agent definitions (JSON) loaded from .amazonq/cli-agents/; workspace-scoped agents

global.notes: Amazon Q CLI does not discover skills from ~/.agents/skills/; uses ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents/ and .amazonq/rules/ instead

local.notes: Amazon Q CLI does not discover skills from .agents/skills/; uses .amazonq/cli-agents/ and .amazonq/rules/ instead

env.name: COPILOT_HOME
env.description: Overrides the main Copilot home/config root.

env.name: COPILOT_CACHE_HOME
env.description: Overrides the Copilot cache root.

installations.notes: Installation method not documented in listed sources.

global.notes: Skills stored in ~/.copilot/skills/ (user) and ~/.agents/skills/ (Agent Skills standard). Skills auto-trigger based on description field or can be invoked as slash commands.

local.notes: Project skills in .github/skills/ and .agents/skills/ are auto-loaded. Skills are invoked as /skill-name slash commands.

global.notes: Skills are invocable as slash commands (/skill-name). Also supports ~/.copilot/instructions/*.instructions.md for user-level custom instructions. Plugin commands are translated into skills.

local.notes: Skills are invocable as slash commands (/skill-name). Also supports .github/instructions/*.instructions.md for pattern-specific custom instructions.

global.notes: Custom agent definitions (.agent.md files) from ~/.copilot/agents/. Invoked via /agent slash command or --agent flag.

local.notes: Custom agent definitions (.agent.md files) from .github/agents/ in the repository. Nested .github/agents directories in subdirectories are also discovered.

global.notes: Support for .agents/skills directory for auto-loading skills was added in v0.0.401. Personal home (~/.agents) skills are loaded alongside ~/.copilot/skills.

local.notes: Support for .agents/skills directory for auto-loading skills was added in v0.0.401.
